zotero.abstractNote: In this paper, we propose a novel similarity measurement for trajectories, the Generalized Multiple Correlation Coefficient (GMCC). The GMCC is based on the canonical correlation analysis and is able to capture linear and non-linear dependencies between trajectories. We show that the GMCC is invariant to affine transformations, which is a desirable property for trajectory similarity measurements. We evaluate the GMCC on several synthetic and real-world datasets and show that it outperforms existing similarity measurements in terms of accuracy and robustness.
zotero.conferenceName: International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems (IROS)
